Step 3: Draw the polygon by connecting the points where the perpendicular bisectors intersect the sides of the triangle. These points will be the vertices of the polygon. Since the polygon is symmetrical about the point \(P\), it will be a regular polygon if the triangle is equilateral.

Step 4: Calculate the area of the polygon. The area of a regular polygon can be calculated using the formula \(A = \frac{1}{2} \times \text{perimeter} \times \text{apothem}\).
